quizzes.constraints: Focus on architecture and production trade-offs.

questions.question: What is a key consideration when transitioning to agentic AI systems in terms of their decision-making architecture?
options: Centralized control with a single decision-maker
Decentralized control with multiple decision-makers and conflict resolution mechanisms
Hierarchical control with a top-down decision-making approach
Distributed control with a focus on individual agent autonomy

questions.question: Which of the following is a potential trade-off when implementing agentic AI systems in production environments?
options: Increased efficiency vs. reduced flexibility
Improved scalability vs. decreased reliability
Enhanced autonomy vs. increased explainability
Better adaptability vs. reduced security

questions.question: What is a common challenge when transitioning to agentic AI systems in terms of their ability to learn and adapt?
options: Overfitting to a specific task or environment
Underfitting to a complex or dynamic environment
Inability to generalize across different contexts
Insufficient data to support effective learning

questions.question: Which of the following is a key factor to consider when designing the reward functions for agentic AI systems?
options: Alignment with human values and goals
Optimization for short-term gains or profits
Flexibility to adapt to changing environments
Scalability to support large numbers of agents

questions.question: What is a potential pitfall when implementing agentic AI systems in complex, dynamic environments?
options: Inability to handle conflicting goals or objectives
Difficulty in scaling to support large numbers of agents
Inadequate consideration of contextual factors and nuances
Insufficient testing and validation of the system

questions.question: Which of the following is a key consideration when transitioning to agentic AI systems in terms of their ability to interact with humans?
options: Designing interfaces that are easy to use and understand
Developing algorithms that can accurately interpret human intent
Implementing mechanisms for transparent and explainable decision-making
Ensuring that the system is robust to human error or variability

questions.question: What is a potential trade-off when implementing agentic AI systems in production environments in terms of their ability to learn and adapt?
options: Increased adaptability vs. decreased robustness
Improved efficiency vs. reduced flexibility
Enhanced autonomy vs. increased explainability
Better scalability vs. decreased reliability

questions.question: Which of the following is a key factor to consider when designing the architecture for agentic AI systems?
options: Modularity and flexibility to support changing requirements
Scalability to support large numbers of agents and complex environments
Robustness to handle failures or anomalies in the system
Explainability to support transparent and accountable decision-making

questions.question: What is a potential challenge when transitioning to agentic AI systems in terms of their ability to handle complex, dynamic environments?
options: Inability to handle conflicting goals or objectives
Difficulty in scaling to support large numbers of agents
Inadequate consideration of contextual factors and nuances
Insufficient testing and validation of the system

questions.question: Which of the following is a key consideration when implementing agentic AI systems in production environments in terms of their ability to interact with humans?
options: Designing interfaces that are easy to use and understand
Developing algorithms that can accurately interpret human intent
Implementing mechanisms for transparent and explainable decision-making
Ensuring that the system is robust to human error or variability

questions.question: In a typical CDC pipeline using Kafka Connect, which component is responsible for converting the raw change events from the source database into a format suitable for downstream consumers?
options: Source connector
Sink connector
Single Message Transform (SMT)
Kafka Streams application

questions.question: When designing a CDC pipeline that must guarantee exactly‑once delivery to a downstream data warehouse, which combination of features is essential?
options: At‑least‑once semantics in the source connector and idempotent writes in the sink
Transactional writes in Kafka and exactly‑once semantics in the sink connector
Log compaction on the Kafka topic and periodic snapshotting of the source database
Batch processing of change events and manual deduplication logic

questions.question: A CDC pipeline must handle schema evolution (e.g., adding a new column). Which strategy minimizes disruption to downstream consumers?
options: Drop and recreate the Kafka topic whenever the schema changes
Emit a separate "schema change" event and require consumers to restart
Use a schema registry to version the Avro/Protobuf schema and allow consumers to read the latest version
Encode the entire row as a JSON string each time, ignoring the schema

questions.question: Which of the following is the most common cause of out‑of‑order events in a CDC pipeline that reads from MySQL binlog?
options: Multiple source connectors reading the same binlog concurrently
Parallel processing of events in the sink without preserving transaction boundaries
Using row‑based replication instead of statement‑based replication
Enabling GTID mode on the MySQL server

questions.question: In a CDC pipeline that uses Debezium as the source connector, what is the purpose of the "heartbeat" topic?
options: To store periodic snapshots of the source database for recovery
To provide a regular signal that the connector is alive and to advance Kafka offsets for idle tables
To replicate the MySQL binary log file names for auditing
To broadcast schema changes to all downstream consumers

questions.question: When scaling a CDC pipeline horizontally, which element must be partitioned to ensure that all changes for a given primary key are processed in order?
options: The Kafka topic's number of partitions based on the primary key hash
The source connector's internal thread pool
The sink connector's batch size configuration
The schema registry's versioning scheme

questions.question: A CDC pipeline is experiencing high latency during peak load. Which tuning action is most likely to reduce end‑to‑end latency without sacrificing data integrity?
options: Increase the Kafka topic retention period
Enable log compaction on the CDC topic
Raise the "max.poll.records" setting in the source connector and reduce the commit interval
Switch from row‑based to statement‑based change capture

questions.question: Which error‑handling pattern is recommended when a sink connector fails to write a batch of CDC records due to a temporary downstream outage?
options: Drop the failed batch and continue with the next one
Move the entire topic to a dead‑letter queue and stop the pipeline
Pause the connector, retry the batch with exponential backoff, and resume once successful
Rewrite the failed records with a different primary key to avoid conflict

questions.question: In a CDC pipeline that streams changes to a data lake in Parquet format, why is it advisable to include the change event's "commit timestamp" as a partition column?
options: It enables time‑travel queries and simplifies incremental loading
It reduces the size of each Parquet file automatically
It allows Kafka to reorder messages based on timestamps
It eliminates the need for a schema registry

questions.question: Which of the following statements best describes the trade‑off between using a log‑based CDC approach versus a trigger‑based CDC approach?
options: Log‑based CDC provides lower latency but higher CPU usage on the source database
Trigger‑based CDC captures schema changes automatically, while log‑based CDC does not
Log‑based CDC is non‑intrusive and can capture all committed transactions, whereas trigger‑based CDC adds overhead to each DML operation
Trigger‑based CDC works only with NoSQL databases, while log‑based CDC works only with relational databases

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a quiz?
options: To assess prior knowledge of a subject
To provide a fun and engaging learning experience
To evaluate the effectiveness of a teaching method
To generate random trivia questions

questions.question: Which of the following is a common type of quiz question?
options: Multiple-choice question
True or False question
Essay question
Fill-in-the-blank question

questions.question: What is the term for a quiz question that requires a short written answer?
options: Short-answer question
Essay question
Fill-in-the-blank question
Multiple-choice question

questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit of using quizzes in education?
options: Improved student motivation
Increased student anxiety
Better student retention
More effective teaching methods

questions.question: What is the term for a quiz question that requires the student to select the correct answer from a list of options?
options: Multiple-choice question
True or False question
Essay question
Fill-in-the-blank question

questions.question: Which of the following is a common type of quiz question that requires the student to evaluate the truth of a statement?
options: True or False question
Multiple-choice question
Essay question
Fill-in-the-blank question

questions.question: What is the term for a quiz question that requires the student to provide a short written answer to a question?
options: Short-answer question
Essay question
Fill-in-the-blank question
Multiple-choice question

questions.question: Which of the following is a benefit of using quizzes in assessment?
options: Improved student motivation
Increased student anxiety
Better student retention
More accurate assessment of student knowledge

questions.question: What is the term for a quiz question that requires the student to complete a sentence or phrase with a missing word?
options: Fill-in-the-blank question
Multiple-choice question
True or False question
Essay question

questions.question: Which of the following is a common type of quiz question that requires the student to select the correct answer from a list of options, but with a twist?
options: True or False question
Multiple-choice question
Essay question
Fill-in-the-blank question

questions.question: What is the primary function of the Big Five personality traits?
options: To identify a person's intelligence quotient
To categorize people based on their social status
To describe a person's patterns of thoughts, feelings, and behaviors
To predict a person's career choices

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an introverted personality trait?
options: Being outgoing and sociable
Being reserved and preferring to work alone
Being highly competitive
Being highly creative

questions.question: What is the term for a person's consistent patterns of thoughts, feelings, and behaviors?
options: Personality type
Personality trait
Personality disorder
Personality profile

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of an individual with a Type A personality?
options: Being highly competitive and perfectionistic
Being highly cooperative and laid-back
Being highly creative and spontaneous
Being highly analytical and detail-oriented

questions.question: What is the term for the study of personality, including its measurement and development?
options: Personality psychology
Social psychology
Developmental psychology
Cognitive psychology

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a personality disorder?
options: Avoidant personality disorder
Borderline personality disorder
Schizoid personality disorder
All of the above

questions.question: What is the term for a person's self-concept, including their attitudes, values, and beliefs?
options: Self-esteem
Self-awareness
Self-concept
Self-actualization

questions.question: Which of the following is a characteristic of an individual with a Type B personality?
options: Being highly competitive and perfectionistic
Being highly cooperative and laid-back
Being highly creative and spontaneous
Being highly analytical and detail-oriented

questions.question: What is the term for the process of developing a person's personality through experience and learning?
options: Personality development
Personality formation
Personality growth
Personality change

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of a personality test?
options: Myers-Briggs Type Indicator (MBTI)
Big Five personality traits
Minnesota Multiphasic Personality Inventory (MMPI)
All of the above

quizzes.constraints: Focus on archietechture and use-case basis

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of the CDC Pipeline in a data processing architecture?
options: To provide real-time data processing capabilities
To handle batch processing of large datasets
To enable data streaming and event-driven processing
To serve as a data warehousing solution

questions.question: Which of the following is a key benefit of using a CDC (Change Data Capture) Pipeline?
options: Improved data consistency and accuracy
Enhanced data security and compliance
Increased data processing speed and efficiency
Better data integration and interoperability

questions.question: In a CDC Pipeline, what is the role of the 'source' component?
options: To capture and process changes to the data
To store and manage the data
To transform and format the data
To deliver the data to the target system

questions.question: What is the primary advantage of using a CDC Pipeline in a data integration scenario?
options: Reduced data latency and increased real-time processing capabilities
Improved data quality and reduced data inconsistencies
Enhanced data security and compliance
Increased data processing speed and efficiency

questions.question: In a CDC Pipeline, what is the purpose of the 'target' component?
options: To capture and process changes to the data
To store and manage the data
To transform and format the data
To deliver the data to the target system

questions.question: Which of the following is a common use case for a CDC Pipeline?
options: Real-time analytics and reporting
Data warehousing and business intelligence
Data integration and ETL (Extract, Transform, Load)
Data security and compliance

questions.question: In a CDC Pipeline, what is the role of the 'transform' component?
options: To capture and process changes to the data
To store and manage the data
To transform and format the data
To deliver the data to the target system

questions.question: What is the primary challenge in designing a CDC Pipeline?
options: Data latency and real-time processing capabilities
Data quality and consistency
Data security and compliance
Data integration and interoperability

questions.question: In a CDC Pipeline, what is the purpose of the 'sink' component?
options: To capture and process changes to the data
To store and manage the data
To transform and format the data
To deliver the data to the target system

questions.question: Which of the following is a key consideration when selecting a CDC Pipeline technology?
options: Scalability and performance
Data security and compliance
Data integration and interoperability
Cost and licensing
